target_profile.intended_use: Internal scientific review of mercury food-contaminant evidence with explicit methylmercury and inorganic-mercury dietary provenance.
notes: This profile supports review-oriented mercury source lookup and documentation without implying a native mercury submission engine.

notes: Owner signoff packet is a reviewer-facing overlay on top of the owner remediation packet and does not mutate readiness, queue, board, handoff, or dossier state.
Waived actions remain explicitly visible so downstream reviewers can inspect accepted deviations from the default owner remediation sequence.
Signed-off status does not imply submission readiness, regulatory approval, or closure of the underlying scientific issue outside the tracked owner lane.
Legal-limit support reviews remain attached to the owner signoff packet so signoff cannot be mistaken for exact jurisdiction coverage when support is partial, anchor-only, or absent.
Illustrative owner-scoped signoff packet derived from scientific follow-up remediation.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Frequency of consumption of different species of fish, crustaceans and molluscs and the effectiveness of national advice in relation to mercury contamination
notes: EFSA published this official annex on 12 February 2026 covering the frequency of consumption of different fish, crustacean, and mollusc species and the effectiveness of national advice related to mercury contamination.
Used in Dietary MCP as current official context for species-sensitive fish and seafood review, especially for high consumers and pregnancy-related advisory questions.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Scientific Opinion on the risk for public health related to the presence of mercury and methylmercury in food
notes: EFSA adopted the mercury and methylmercury opinion on 22 November 2012 and published it on 20 December 2012.
The opinion established a TWI of 1.3 micrograms per kilogram body weight per week for methylmercury and 4 micrograms per kilogram body weight per week for inorganic mercury.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Metals as contaminants in food
notes: Current EFSA topic page summarising risk assessments and legal context for arsenic, cadmium, lead, mercury and other metals as contaminants in food.
Used in Dietary MCP as a maintained official context source for metals-contaminant governance.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Commission Regulation (EU) 2023/915 on maximum levels for certain contaminants in food
notes: Binding EU contaminant regulation that includes maximum levels for trace elements, PFAS, and other contaminants in food.
Used in Dietary MCP as a shared EU legal anchor for PFAS, cadmium, lead, and mercury food-contaminant governance.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Commission Regulation (EC) No 333/2007 laying down the methods of sampling and analysis for the official control of the levels of trace elements and processing contaminants in foodstuffs
notes: Current consolidated EU official-control regulation covering sampling and analysis requirements for trace elements and processing contaminants in foodstuffs.
Used in Dietary MCP as the shared official-control source anchor for cadmium, lead, inorganic arsenic, and mercury food-contaminant governance.

emerging_contaminant_snapshot.display_name: Mercury food contaminants and dietary exposure watchlist
notes: This family is exposed as a governed mercury food contaminants and dietary exposure watchlist with explicit EFSA and EU legal provenance.
Submission-oriented use is allowed when the dossier meets standard governance and fingerprint requirements.

limitations.message: Owner-lane signoff dossier uses version-pinned hashes but is not cryptographically signed.
Owner-lane signoff dossier records audit-ready follow-up, waiver, and escalation posture only.

notes: Version-pinned owner signoff dossier captures the exact owner-lane signoff packet, source dossier payload, and release metadata used for downstream audit.
Escalation items are derived only from explicit waivers and unresolved blocking actions recorded in the owner signoff packet.
This dossier is an owner-lane audit overlay and does not create a new readiness state, submission engine, or final regulatory decision package.

Legal-limit support reviews are pinned on the owner-lane dossier so downstream audit can distinguish exact curated support from partial, anchor-only, or explicit-gap posture without cross-jurisdiction borrowing.
